name: Ebro Nature Trail (GR 99): San Adrián Loop
name_original: GR99, río Ega y Ebro – circular desde San Adrián
description: Enjoy an easy 3.9-mile (6.3 km) circular hike along the Río Ega and Ebro from San Adrián, taking about 1 hour and 35 minutes.
region_intro: Hiking around San Adrián offers a diverse landscape shaped by the Ebro River, providing a mix of riverside paths and agricultural terrain. The region features gentle elevation changes, making it suitable for various fitness levels. Many trails traverse fertile plains and offer views of the surrounding countryside. These routes provide opportunities to explore the natural environment of San Adrián. Best hiking trails in San Adrián The most popular hiking route is Embalse de El Perdiguero — circular desde Calahorra, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding fields. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ebro Nature Trail (GR 99): San Adrián Loop, an easy 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path. This route follows the Ebro River, providing flat, accessible terrain. Local hikers also love the Río Ebro to Mirador de Azagra – circular from Azagra, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes and agricultural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around San Adrián is defined by its Ebro River paths, agricultural fields, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging routes with moderate climbs. The routes in San Adrián are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore San Adrián's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around San Adrián?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes around San Adrián, offering a variety of experiences. The region is characterized by its Ebro River paths and agricultural landscapes, with options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in San Adrián?
A: Yes, San Adrián offers many easy trails. More than 20 routes are classified as easy. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111830742/ebro-nature-trail-gr-99-san-adrian-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Nature Trail (GR 99): San Adrián Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.9 miles (6.3 km) and follows the Ebro River on flat, accessible terrain.

Q: Are there any circular walks in the San Adrián area?
A: Yes, many trails around San Adrián are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246173419/embalse-de-el-perdiguero-circular-desde-calahorra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de El Perdiguero — circular desde Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 9.0-mile (14.4 km) loop offering views of the reservoir. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246191784/san-adrian-by-the-rio-ebro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián by the Río Ebro — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.7-mile (5.9 km) route.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around San Adrián?
A: Hikes around San Adrián typically feature paths along the Ebro River, agricultural fields, and gentle hills. The elevation changes are generally mild, making most routes suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of riverside strolls and routes through cultivated landscapes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to see near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perdiguero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature, and you might also consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town&lt;/a&gt; or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalén Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which are within the broader region.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult hikes in San Adrián?
A: While most trails are easy to moderate, there is at least one difficult route for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246232058/san-adrian-and-camino-natural-del-ebro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián and Camino Natural del Ebro Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.0-mile (21.0 km) hike with more significant elevation changes, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in San Adrián?
A: The hiking routes in San Adrián are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths along the Ebro River and the peaceful agricultural scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Ebro River?
A: Many routes in San Adrián follow the Ebro River, providing scenic riverside views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111125107/rio-ebro-to-mirador-de-azagra-circular-from-azagra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Ebro to Mirador de Azagra – circular from Azagra&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.6-mile (12.2 km) trail that leads through riverside landscapes and agricultural areas, offering excellent river vistas.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in San Adrián?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111830742/ebro-nature-trail-gr-99-san-adrian-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Nature Trail (GR 99): San Adrián Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes, while longer, moderate routes might take around 3-4 hours. The most challenging trails can extend to over 5 hours.

Q: Is San Adrián a good place for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, San Adrián is suitable for family-friendly hikes, especially due to its many easy routes and gentle terrain. The riverside paths are generally flat and accessible, making them ideal for families with children or those looking for a relaxed stroll.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by a reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246173419/embalse-de-el-perdiguero-circular-desde-calahorra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de El Perdiguero — circular desde Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; route specifically offers views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perdiguero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing a pleasant waterside hiking experience.
tips:
San Adrián is a small city with less than 7,000 inhabitants. It was probably built due to its location. It is where the Ega and Ebro rivers meet. This makes for a nice nature walk along the GR99 alongside the river. The city also has some interesting buildings such as the Old Parish of the Virgen de la Palma and the
Parish of San Adrian.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the GR99, río EGA and Ebro – circular desde San Adrián?
faqs.answer: This easy circular route typically takes about 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate for continuous hiking and does not include stops for breaks or sightseeing.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, with its easy difficulty grade, minimal elevation changes (around 17 meters up and down), and relatively short distance of approximately 6.26 km, this route is very suitable for beginners and families looking for a pleasant outdoor experience near San Adrián.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in San Adrián. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a town, San Adrián typically offers public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat with very little elevation gain, making it an easy walk. Given its proximity to the Ebro and Ega rivers, expect paths that are likely well-maintained and suitable for a leisurely hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the natural scenery along the rivers is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially in the Ebro valley, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable then. Winter can also be pleasant on clear days.

faqs.question: Is the GR99, río EGA and Ebro – circular desde San Adrián dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife, and remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the GR99, río EGA and Ebro – circular desde San Adrián. It's a publicly accessible route.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino natural de Ebro - Tramo central and the Corredor Verde del Ebro, offering connections to broader regional trail networks.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary natural features are the Ega and Ebro rivers themselves, offering scenic views of the riverbanks and surrounding agricultural landscapes. The route provides a pleasant perspective of the confluence area and the town of San Adrián.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or pubs near the trail in San Adrián?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in San Adrián, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and pubs within the town where you can refresh yourself before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty and short distance, essential items include comfortable walking shoes, water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light snack. Depending on the weather, a light jacket might also be useful.
